name: Tom Perez
subject: Now we throw them out of office
disclaimer_text: paid for by cutting working families off from the care they need
body:
Tonight's victory
---
**The fight is far from over -- now let's make sure we throw these Republicans out of office. Chip in $3 or more.

** **[DONATE](http://my.democrats.org/page/m/405232f7/4067b0a/305e45f7f/7e6a8099/1337069101/VEsH/ "https://my.democrats.org/Elect-Democrats-2017-AB?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20170717_tp_nd&source=em_20170717_tp_nd&refcode=em_20170717_tp_nd")**

Derek --

Tonight, because of millions of grassroots Democrats like you who banded together and fought against it, Republicans' attempt to take health care away from millions of Americans failed again.

While this is a victory for basic human decency, for the families who rely on the Affordable Care Act, and for all of us who believe that health care is a right for all Americans, the fight is far from over.

Republicans are determined to take away health care from millions. We saw it when Donald Trump, Mitch McConnell, and Senate Republicans worked in secret, behind closed doors, to write a bill that would have given massive tax cuts to millionaires and billionaires, paid for by cutting working families off from the care they need.

They ignored the overwhelming opposition of their constituents -- and now we're going to make sure their constituents throw them out of office.

Help build the foundation to take on these Republicans in every state, elect Democrats who will protect every American's access to health care, and take back our majorities in Congress. Chip in $3 or more tonight.

Senate Republicans are exempting themselves from a key measure of their health care repeal bill. That's absurd.
---
**[](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954dbcb/2b07114c/3dff465b/73113fdc/2315600754/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/no-exemption-for-republicans?source=PT-20170717-Signature_DS-ProsHV&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=")**

Team,

**Senate Republicans want to exempt themselves from their own health care bill.**

You read that right. Senate Republicans want to pass a bill that would cause people to pay more for less coverage -- and rack up medical bills or worse, go without treatment -- but they want to keep their Obamacare.

[**Stand with Democrats who are telling Republicans to find a backbone -- either accept the consequences of your own health care bill or don 't pass it. Add your name now. **](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954dbcb/2b07114c/3dff465b/73113fdc/2315600754/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/no-exemption-for-republicans?source=PT-20170717-Signature_DS-ProsHV&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=")

It's disgusting for Senate Republicans to worry about protecting their own health care while trying to rip coverage away from 22 million Americans -- and raise costs for millions more!

And it's no wonder they don't want this bill for themselves! Their Obamacare repeal would allow insurers to sell plans that don't cover essential benefits, like emergency room visits, maternity care and cancer screenings.

**Republicans should know that if this bill isn 't good enough for them, it's not good enough for the American people either. Add your name now: **

[**http://www.dscc.org/no-health-care-exemption**](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954dbcb/2b07114c/3dff465b/73113fdc/2315600754/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWYVNWQWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/no-exemption-for-republicans?source=PT-20170717-Signature_DS-ProsHV&firstname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=")

Team DSCC

---|---|---|---
| | |
# Why I'm Fighting to Protect Health Care
The fight on health care continues to be front and center in the U.S. Senate. Senate Republicans have unveiled a so-called 'fix' to their 'health care' bill. But in reality, they only made it worse. It now includes a provision from Senator Ted Cruz that allows insurance companies to segregate the market into those who feel they are invincible (they can purchase cheaper "junk insurance" plans only to discover their insurance doesn't cover their unexpected needs) from those who expect to need decent coverage. It will lead to huge, unaffordable price hikes for anyone with pre-existing conditions and, even the insurance companies concede, it would throw much of the insurance market into a death spiral. And the rest of the bill is still rotten to its core. It guts patient protections - even those who purchase insurance through their employer will risk losing protections. It still kicks millions of kids, seniors, and struggling families in Maryland and across the country off of Medicaid, and it would cap the care for those who are able to still receive coverage - especially people with disabilities. And it turns back the progress we've made in fighting the opioid epidemic - all while giving big tax breaks to powerful corporations. This week, I met with a family from Maryland who lost their son and brother, Jaime, to an opioid overdose. He was a proud University of Maryland Terp, a special education teacher, and an amazing cook with a kind heart. I also recently had the opportunity to visit Beacon of Hope Recovery Center in St. Mary's County to talk with health care professionals, law enforcement officials, and Marylanders recovering from addiction about how important it is to treat addiction like the dangerous health issue that it is. I am so grateful to my fellow Marylanders, who are willing to share their stories in the fight to protect our health care system. At the end of the day, I hope stories like these will help my Republican colleagues realize how misguided and damaging their health care plan is. I stand ready to work on a bipartisan basis to improve our health care system - but I will fight tooth and nail against any effort to take health care away from families who need it the most in order to give tax breaks to big corporations and special interests. Sincerely, Chris Van Hollen United States Senator **UPDATES: ** **Confronting the Danger Posed by North Korea** ****With North Korea now capable of launching an intercontinental ballistic missile that could reach Alaska, it has crossed a critical and dangerous threshold \-- and the United States must take action. That 's why Senator Pat Toomey and I introduced bipartisan legislation that will fill an important gap in our current sanctions regime against North Korea by going after the foreign banks and firms that have provided illicit support to Kim Jong Un. It will send a clear and unequivocal message: the economic consequences will be severe for those who continue to do business with North Korea. Our goal is to achieve a Korean Peninsula free of nuclear weapons. [](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14336.6083673.3449279.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.facebook.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2fvideos%2f10155016975618878%2f&redir_log=73451911224405) **************Victories for Maryland in Military Construction-VA Appropriations Bill** ** ** As a member of the Senate Appropriations Committee, I'm working hard to ensure critical priorities for Maryland and our nation have the support they need. This week, I was proud to support the Fiscal Year 2018 Military Construction, Veterans Affairs (VA), and Related Agencies appropriations bill, which includes hundred of millions of dollars for critical housing, infrastructure, and facilities for U.S. military forces and their families, as well as increased funding for veterans' health care and benefits. Maryland is proud to support so many military installations and to have so many military personnel and veterans call our state home, and I will keep fighting to ensure they have the support they need on and off the battlefield. For a full list of projects funded in this legislation, click [here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=14336.6083673.3449279.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fcontent%2fvan-hollen-fights-maryland-military-construction-va-appropriations-bill&redir_log=876552975398799).

clean_body: | | ** | | | July 14, 2017 | ---|---|---|--- | | | Why I'm Fighting to Protect Health Care The fight on health care continues to be front and center in the U.S. Senate. Senate Republicans have unveiled a so-called 'fix' to their 'health care' bill. But in reality, they only made it worse. It now includes a provision from Senator Ted Cruz that allows insurance companies to segregate the market into those who feel they are invincible (they can purchase cheaper "junk insurance" plans only to discover their insurance doesn't cover their unexpected needs) from those who expect to need decent coverage. It will lead to huge, unaffordable price hikes for anyone with pre-existing conditions and, even the insurance companies concede, it would throw much of the insurance market into a death spiral. And the rest of the bill is still rotten to its core. It guts patient protections - even those who purchase insurance through their employer will risk losing protections. It still kicks millions of kids, seniors, and struggling families in Maryland and across the country off of Medicaid, and it would cap the care for those who are able to still receive coverage - especially people with disabilities. And it turns back the progress we've made in fighting the opioid epidemic - all while giving big tax breaks to powerful corporations. This week, I met with a family from Maryland who lost their son and brother, Jaime, to an opioid overdose. He was a proud University of Maryland Terp, a special education teacher, and an amazing cook with a kind heart. I also recently had the opportunity to visit Beacon of Hope Recovery Center in St. Mary's County to talk with health care professionals, law enforcement officials, and Marylanders recovering from addiction about how important it is to treat addiction like the dangerous health issue that it is. I am so grateful to my fellow Marylanders, who are willing to share their stories in the fight to protect our health care system. At the end of the day, I hope stories like these will help my Republican colleagues realize how misguided and damaging their health care plan is. I stand ready to work on a bipartisan basis to improve our health care system - but I will fight tooth and nail against any effort to take health care away from families who need it the most in order to give tax breaks to big corporations and special interests. Sincerely, Chris Van Hollen United States Senator UPDATES: Confronting the Danger Posed by North Korea With North Korea now capable of launching an intercontinental ballistic missile that could reach Alaska, it has crossed a critical and dangerous threshold \-- and the United States must take action. That 's why Senator Pat Toomey and I introduced bipartisan legislation that will fill an important gap in our current sanctions regime against North Korea by going after the foreign banks and firms that have provided illicit support to Kim Jong Un. It will send a clear and unequivocal message: the economic consequences will be severe for those who continue to do business with North Korea. Our goal is to achieve a Korean Peninsula free of nuclear weapons. **********Victories for Maryland in Military Construction-VA Appropriations Bill As a member of the Senate Appropriations Committee, I'm working hard to ensure critical priorities for Maryland and our nation have the support they need. This week, I was proud to support the Fiscal Year 2018 Military Construction, Veterans Affairs (VA), and Related Agencies appropriations bill, which includes hundred of millions of dollars for critical housing, infrastructure, and facilities for U.S. military forces and their families, as well as increased funding for veterans' health care and benefits. Maryland is proud to support so many military installations and to have so many military personnel and veterans call our state home, and I will keep fighting to ensure they have the support they need on and off the battlefield. name: The White House
subject: Fighting for American workers and families
body:
President Trump hosted 50 companies from each state to showcase American products as part of Made in America Week.
| | [](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab341d2b37a6076df457a857dd11b7a939715b65a53b492a664ca90bd4291fd99c98c2fffe303755135e14a17e87a87f05b)Yesterday, President Donald J. Trump hosted 50 companies from each state to [showcase American products as part of Made in America Week, ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab33d95d52ff08598d5afe6cc51e55ed2a6170b2c4d1e6d129fd5ff9e38231f3ab531564cfd174f9b5916b7da51a0d3f1e4)**reaffirming his dedication to fight on behalf of American workers and families.** Products ranged from sandwiches, to wool blankets, to firetrucks; each business connected by the common thread of American excellence. The President stated that, "When we purchase products made in the USA, the profits stay here, the revenue stays here, and the jobs -- maybe most importantly of all -- they stay right here in the USA".
# Today's Events
**10:10 AM:** Vice President Pence delivers the keynote address at the National Retail Federation’s Annual Retail Advocates Summit**10:30 AM:** President Trump receives his daily intelligence briefing**11:00 AM:** President Trump meets with National Security Advisor H.R. McMaster**11:30 AM:** President Trump speaks with Sultan Qaboos bin Said al Said of Oman by telephone**12:30 PM:** President Trump and Vice President Pence have lunch with service members**1:15 PM:** Vice President Pence participates in the Senate Republican Policy Lunch**2:00 PM:** President Trump attends a Cabinet Affairs meeting**2:00 PM:** Press Briefing with Principal Deputy Press Secretary Sarah Sanders**2:15 PM:** Vice President Pence participates in a series of meetings with lawmakers**4:30 PM:** Vice President Pence participates in a life insurers roundtable
# Photo of the Day
**Made in America Product Showcase** | July 17, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Evan Walker)
# OBAMACARE FACT OF THE DAY
**FACT:** In Arizona, average premiums are [up $4,800](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab3de6db5b23f8adaaf7e5937a9448b58f03a889eb9c181cca3ce255bfccb0b44c0691b2e3c95cc3a709bfe39fab9d5ccf3) since Obamacare was implemented.[](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab3de6db5b23f8adaaf7e5937a9448b58f03a889eb9c181cca3ce255bfccb0b44c0691b2e3c95cc3a709bfe39fab9d5ccf3)
# WHITE HOUSE UPDATES
Yesterday, President Trump kicked off the [Made In America Showcase](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab30d1b2e78ebdbc760196a00bcfbe943598e9ec06740182c2041059cdd2d145be849ed695f19f133375ae78bb7292ec493) at the White House as a part of [Made In America Week.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab329790315c64cd5749bc8366aea70f44152d62f290ee0dcd4fec998179c2d575ec5903207417a328477f2cbf957729c75) The event displayed products from all 50 states that are "made in America", the world standard for quality and craftsmanship. [](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab383bf7f65da594b1f72183365833765a200d689137d08ea7cd2975f925415bd7a08e6ef49dc4ba11ce0bfe5f9d2f29598) To see more photos from the Made In America event, checkout our [Flickr](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab3d9704063deab04135b900f4a1b74c030d7474b11058869278e586de6b5b24543a0efcf801f7024809d491095635e50e4) and [Facebook](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab3d4a7f4c20c99ad45eeffe0d63c32baf39943ce8ad05ee388a0e45698903329756de4bac3a2d3fa5228ab5f60582fb809). [](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab3f16bb47ff5ec64563e1f87be9cfb95dd9f4c4ee2911ff032d6224611f92d859e71fa4d0eb69690023c4968345d839a64)Yesterday, Vice President Pence [hosted Serbian President Aleksandar Vučić.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab39b952f2f7db783aec026ae06fc41278075a1cfbe37223a21ffe8401753996916fb79e2054974184b9e8520b34ec7dfb0)[](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab3612fb77ecabc32988b3522b19f579beeb2cf08950975e0dcfc96139bdc53880c8af32f4d52ac613c2df47d4143faf167)Last night, Vice President Pence gave [remarks](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab30590a825d17f51594dc4f89ca6f973fe5d1729f2c9b39b43a2c30316c464d5a9e0bda06b0892c27e3239cd2099426b52) at a Christians United for Israel event [](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab3588a0f9db2d21b90b5a97c5be9b1b8ceeaa521a0984e7fff84abb9975b12861cb198abe0f4a9dc1b4e2cc1d08fd84cea)Last night, President Trump [issued a statement](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab330f2017d5f5a203605af5fbd487b86222a5b5532936ab5ab25b6c4dc4781242f1ccfcc5a89ada06c508678ae6c246a4f) regarding the situation in Venezuela.
# West Wing Reads
Each day we put together a list of the top news stories the West Wing is reading and share them with you. **[Sign up](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab3632d409f42cace10e1135c17e55461d6d61f5925750d55e5fe2558ec820510278e9b7b79dc9b403ec47180a6d9d25311) to receive this daily update from the West Wing.**[](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab3632d409f42cace10e1135c17e55461d6d61f5925750d55e5fe2558ec820510278e9b7b79dc9b403ec47180a6d9d25311)
---

[Privacy Policy](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab321a5d44acd704ef926cdc251da4a2a754d412b190509a6fb2464b7dc43c7a6e1f23178f410ac3dad71b418118ebd5e43) | [Contact the White House](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=a8af2d324154aab325866e25e94fa146f17cce7143ae30f85d414e82ec22fc994926a24cc8663a51193988b8a8c7fcc639e4833bdb378736) | [Unsubscribe](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d449afce69925d2ad30cc0ffe9b68036078d2eafbe1bef837c9df33fede2a8af879e313fa9cd9ae6b8703696c7a3875d25)

name: Roy Cooper
subject: It's time to Break the Majority
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
|
[](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1202d60/2fde7c66/42ca1f2d/15696910/4030010744/VEsH/ "http://www.breakthemajority.com?utm_campaign=em17_170718_breakthemajority_acq____RC&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Cooper&source=em17_170718_breakthemajority_acq____RC&utm_content=1")
---
Friends,

On the trail last year, people would ask me all the time -- what has happened to North Carolina? We once were a beacon in the South, but recently we've been making the national news for all the wrong reasons.

The answer is simple: Partisan gerrymandering.

North Carolina has some of the most gerrymandered districts in the country. It has created a rigged system where the politicians are picking their voters instead of the other way around.

Republicans in the legislature have used super-majorities in both chambers to pass extreme laws that have taken North Carolina backward.

**Today I'm proud to announce Break the Majority -- a new effort to break the Republican super-majorities in the legislature.**

**[Together with the NC Democratic Party, we will recruit strong candidates and support them with one goal in mind: move North Carolina forward. We can't do this without your support -- sign up today to join us!](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1202d60/2fde7c66/42ca1f2d/15696913/4030010744/VEsE/ "http://action.ncdp.org/page/s/break-the-majority?utm_campaign=em17_170718_btm_acq_gq_ful__RC&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Cooper&source=em17_170718_btm_acq_gq_ful__RC&utm_content=2+-+Together+with+the+NC+Democratic+Party+we")**

We can't afford to have Republican super-majorities in the legislature anymore. They passed a voter suppression law that the courts said targeted African-Americans with "surgical precision." They passed HB2. They passed laws restricting women's access to health care. They pass budgets that have drained money from public education and given tax giveaways to the wealthy corporations.

Those aren't North Carolina values. That's not the North Carolina we know and love. We have to fight back now.

Against all odds last year, we worked together to defeat an incumbent governor for the first time. We made history.

Republicans in the legislature have used super-majorities in both chambers to pass extreme laws that have taken North Carolina backward. Today I'm proud to announce Break the Majority -- a new effort to break the Republican super-majorities in the legislature. name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Like Hillary said: this is a critical moment
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
Tell Republicans not to take away affordable health care
[](https://2016.democrats.org/page/m/4052310f/4067b0a/3056719b5/7e6d7734/2014034715/VEsH/ "http://2016.democrats.org/DSCC-ACA?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=PT-20170718-warren-v1s4&source=PT-20170718-warren-v1s4")
---
Derek,

Senate Republicans are still pulling out all the stops, determined to repeal the Affordable Care Act -- and it could happen any day now.

I wish I were crying wolf -- but I'm really worried. Health care for millions of Americans is on the line right now. The Republicans are counting on the fact that so many terrible things are happening -- and that Republicans have put out the word that Trumpcare is on the rocks -- all so they can jam this through while we let down our guard.

Hillary Clinton said it like it is: "This is a critical moment about choosing people over politics. Speak out against this bill."

**[Because you were there for Hillary in 2016, I'm hoping we can count on you to stand with Senate Democrats in a fight that matters so much to her and to all of us. Tell Republicans right now: we will NOT let you repeal health care for tens of millions of Americans.](https://2016.democrats.org/page/m/4052310f/4067b0a/3056719b5/7e6d7732/2014034715/VEsE/ "https://2016.democrats.org/DSCC-ACA?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+Because+you+were+there+for+Hillary+in+20&utm_campaign=PT-20170718-warren-v1s4&source=PT-20170718-warren-v1s4")**

Senate Republicans are still doing all of their deal-making behind closed doors. We have no idea what last-ditch giveaways they are promising to undecided Republicans to secure their votes.

That's right: Senate Democrats still haven't seen a final bill -- and once it's done, we'll likely only have a few days to look at it. Only a few days to see all of the ways that they will gut health care for seniors, for veterans, for children, for people with disabilities -- for working people across this country.

I've never seen anything like this. I've never seen anything close to this. Tens of millions of people affected and one-sixth of our economy on the line, but Senate Republicans say no hearings, no amendments, no debate. This scheme to repeal health care takes every norm of the Senate -- every concept of how we work together -- and just burns them to the ground.

**[Senate Democrats are ready to fight back -- and fight back hard. But we can't do it alone. Please add your name right now to tell Republicans: we won't let you repeal Americans' health care. (We'll even send you a free sticker when you do!)](https://2016.democrats.org/page/m/4052310f/4067b0a/3056719b5/7e6d7730/2014034715/VEsF/ "https://2016.democrats.org/DSCC-ACA?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+Senate+Democrats+are+ready+to+fight+back&utm_campaign=PT-20170718-warren-v1s4&source=PT-20170718-warren-v1s4")**

Derek, we need you to share your story (again). We need you to light up the phone lines (again). We need you to talk to your FOX News-loving brother or uncle about why we need Medicaid, veterans' care, and money to fight the opioid epidemic (again, or for the first time).

This is the time to fight.

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

Click [here](https://2016.democrats.org/page/m/4052310f/4067b0a/3056719b5/7e6d773e/2014034715/VEsC/ "https://2016-dnc.bsd.net/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+here&utm_campaign=PT-20170718-warren-v1s4&source=PT-20170718-warren-v1s4") to unsubscribe.

name: The White House
subject: West Wing Reads
body:
1600 Daily
Made in America Week

Special Edition

Each day we round up the best stories the West Wing is reading and share them with you.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9e78ce8745b31584a232df034bb50f96e981cbb38ce7605899f087e6a2c6b41aa4b361392112564272ddc2e621c1becf4a64bab8dd880129
SIGN UP to continue receiving this update from the West Wing.

------------

The
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9e78ce8745b31584a65a3707c0df24a46351538f1d3fa6d4edfa0e29d40461a793d2b81dd71e0d9d45c0b688fd96241df44736877d4bccf4
Waterloo Cedar Falls Courier featured a locally-owned body armor firm that was invited, and the
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9e78ce8745b3158489af4b2cd45dc95280284698c4af04ec9d80beb3c5ae51a80902511817060a385a322cd67a2247ed519d06e39c81c12f
Asheville Citizen Times reported that “Cheerwine, born in the South, to be raised in the White House.”

------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9e78ce8745b31584c69533e75c3d2bbbf5b2f7c9d5c90bfdecad5df712c7a603191a84f19bba4de28d94ccdd7acf8e2c2f6cfc0c68e71827
The Bradenton Herald highlighted Tervis Tumbler Co., the hardy drinkware manufactured out of North Venice, that was chosen as Florida’s made in America product. “It’s an honor to represent the 700 employees who design, decorate and build Tervis drinkware with American hands and values,” said Rogan Donelly, the company’s president.

------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9e78ce8745b31584d1009099449fe596bfb058f12c1d27256da3c3f71fb44d761412a4b47ded791a8be03c9b7cb2f77f5fbe4188e3ddd485
The Salt Lake Tribune featured the Utah based company, Colonial Flag, and his owner Paul Swenson, as they covered the White House Made in America Showcase. "This has been a great present for our business as far as generally giving hope," said Swenson about his visit to the White House and the exposure he has received. "A lot of what we see, if nothing else, the intent of this president is to give hope for small business."

------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9e78ce8745b31584841337951de56631fbfa558dcd37e1ebeeff86f985acf5efbc3b09cb72c38ec822df0f0e2253d06917246d042f91f6c0
The Hartford Courant wrote about the President’s experience with Sikorsky Helicopters, a Lockheed Martin Company, during Monday’s White House ‘Made in America’ showcase. The Connecticut-based company touted the 7,000 men and women it employs in Connecticut alone, and shared that its plan to upgrade the Presidential Marine One fleet will also include work at facilities in Coatesville, Pennsylvania, and Oswego, New York.

------------

“I brought Narragansett Beer back 12 years ago because I believed then, and even more strongly now, that local and independent business matters,” said the president and CEO Mark Hellendrung of Narragansett, who represented Rhode Island, in a statement issued by
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9e78ce8745b3158472ceb5ed5c7f6d4f6962520f9148f403a0a7dad84106cdbd1eaf298fc0a5233c3fc7537edded8e6427e4b2abdb638970
Providence Journal.

------------

The Great Alaskan Bowl Company, a Fairbanks company, represented Alaska at the White House during the showcase.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9e78ce8745b31584e59b63513f9e70963e132f1d5980ba337021387a98ed3094487d16829704f829cca392f3ee20add78ca3092e05f5237f
The Daily News-Miner quoted the company owner, “This effort was high on the president’s agenda and just to be a part of the event, and be there with everyone from the other states felt like a grassroots experience. I’ve been there from the ground up and everyone else has too...it’s difficult to predict what the showcase will do for the Great Alaskan Bowl Company in terms of business and exposure, but it will be positive.”

------------

In
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9e78ce8745b315845e3df7c177ace41faea203771da867c2e4a8210e8cdd49c60d454fd8d1266daca335247907fbc8d212f2f2a50e3b0a97
The Delmarva Daily Times, the presence of a Somerset County crab pot manufacturer brought residents and local business owners pride, and for some “a sign of hope.” “It’s nice to see something positive in Crisfield,” an eastern-shore Maryland town that has struggled since the 2008 financial crisis and was devastated by Hurricane Sandy in 2012.

------------

Baseball bats from Baton Rouge-based Marucci Sports were among the items that drew the President's attention, wrote
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9e78ce8745b31584130adc6010d5a2e2b06f33faad5bb6b26e49ff09f81754d7b35e6a882350ed261a8c049139a08f363492fbf3773e7e89
The Advocate from Louisiana. Followed by President Trump's quote "We want to build, create, and grow more products in our country using American labor, American goods, and American grit."

------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9e78ce8745b315844f3b14d7298affa578b4495b78f85e170f62e3acbf0cc3ffacd819a6f0bfe592056281da0c22aa547fcde0c4c45adacb
The Dallas Morning News mentioned how President Donald Trump donned a Stetson, the iconic Western hat made in Texas, during the event. Dustin Noblitt, of Stetson hats, told the reporter "We have a hat here especially for him, it's the El Presidente, it's the General Edition and we had it scripted in the sweatband, Donald J. Trump, Make America Great Again 45th President. We have the seal as well,"

------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9e78ce8745b31584f429cd7a0166391991ea77dc670a8dffd141ac2978a5be23fd4d48c7c4d66b53be8a12371dba632080f446c41a2d1c8c
The New Jersey Star Ledger writes that representatives from Camden-based Campbell Soup Company presented an offering of tomato soup and grilled cheese, among other American made products. Campbell executives “applauded the administration's regulatory reductions,” and backed the President’s call to reform the corporate tax rate and renegotiate NAFTA.

------------

>From the Ohio Valley region,
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9e78ce8745b3158402ca2b98c5a6aff0e80cd22f375016f91c69e5ac5fc77b21287da210f3b19c64581be2e62af382df37495b2c16836adb
WTOV explores the ‘made in America’ commitment held by Bully Tools of Ohio, and Homer Laughlin China Company of West Virginia. As an employer of more than 600 people, Homer Laughlin’s ‘made in America’ commitment means income and jobs for the people of Newell, Chester, and East Liverpool. For Bully Tools, local vendors credit the company’s ‘made in America’ craftsmanship for having superior products on the market.

------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c7c7d48d4cc8c6d733087f84ec8cb4e172743bb59212edad178777b7e001c05209ab59973255ae10bff9c6df1a851834f68af4431076fc75
The Commercial Appeal, a Gannett newspaper from Tennessee, reported that President Trump shared the stage with Gibson guitars at the White House event on Monday saying: this administration is “working to remove government regulations and trade barriers so that business so can prosper”

------------

Nevada has more than a few trademarks to boast about, but one in particular found its sweet spot in the “Made in America” product showcase at the White House.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c7c7d48d4cc8c6d71f6b457644a05ff7ae26c823bce61b5e1e376f7caf0838cbb8aa57183fba28f6d3b9cee5af21fb9bad59784775d5e964
The Las Vegas Review Journal highlights that Reno-based Kimmie Candies, makers of ChocoRocks and Kimmie Candy Salted Pretzel Balls, was originally manufacturing outside of the U.S. and returned to Nevada to hire American. To its credit, Kimmie Candy proudly walks the “made in America” walk.

------------

Broomcorn Johnny’s, a craft broom manufacturer from Harrison County, Indiana, was among local representatives from all 50 states at the White House’s ‘Made in America’ event Monday, reports
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c7c7d48d4cc8c6d7b0a0d83c37461b3703ac0401f0140db70ec148a65b6edcb5df58b81bb57ceae6ebaeafbd675601aa4320ef5ad4bb41a7
The Indy Star. The President doffed a Stetson hat made in Texas, practiced his stroke with a golf putter made by the Ping company of Arizona, and swung a baseball bat made by Marucci Sports of Louisiana before formally declaring this week "Made in America Week."

------------

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=9e78ce8745b31584a232df034bb50f96e981cbb38ce7605899f087e6a2c6b41aa4b361392112564272ddc2e621c1becf4a64bab8dd880129
Sign up to continue getting the top news stories the West Wing is reading.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c7c7d48d4cc8c6d73ae23680b670952d4197e8a6e66c944b1369f8ac709a733c78f50e3ab7d0e2d9c76ad7c057d900507db40a71a0b28a18
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=c7c7d48d4cc8c6d71ea7d1c4be3ced5889d065ff2ab3692a0ebf99e62ed7f26d701c27367dfa83e39af579372f5456f3c736132f1dda5231
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d431c08d6f78bd1143b9f5c586fc31696264dafafe20ab31df598c869bd81133e9c416b131845de9468fa4fb5fbbfe3d26
Unsubscribe

name: Nina Turner, Our Revolution
subject: The Summer for Progress
body:
U.S. House Democratic Caucus signed on to cosponsor the following bills
#
Friends,
Yesterday, we scored a huge victory! All across the country, people put their bodies on the line and used their voices to stand up for health care. Senate Republicans do not have the votes to repeal the Affordable Care Act and will _not_ be voting on their disastrous plan to take away health care from 22 million people.
This victory shows that when people come together there is nothing we cannot accomplish. We must build off of this and continue to not just express frustration but provide solutions to fix the issues that too many Americans face today.
This summer, Our Revolution is working with dozens of other grassroots organizations to back the #PeoplesPlatform, a bold, progressive policy agenda that we will be lobbying members of Congress to support during their recess. Our goal is to have the majority of the U.S. House Democratic Caucus signed on to cosponsor the following bills when they return in September:
1. **Health Care for All** : H.R. 676 Medicare for All Act

body:
---
Derek,

Corey Stewart announced last week that he is running against Senator Kaine. He is the pro-Confederate, anti-immigrant local official who nearly won the Republican nomination for Governor a few weeks ago.

And he plans to run "a vicious and ruthless campaign" against Tim. Those are his words.

Corey is prepared to go straight to the gutter in his campaign, with lies, nastiness and constant negativity.

This is the opposite of the Tim we know. He fights for children's cancer research, jobs for veterans, career and technical education, shipbuilding, opioid treatment, a cleaner Chesapeake Bay and good health care for folks in every part of the Commonwealth.

He's deeply respected by his Senate colleagues on both sides of the aisle.

And he does it with an upbeat and can-do spirit that reflects the goodness of Virginians.

Upbeat and positive versus "vicious and ruthless." That's a real choice.

**[Can you support Tim's positive vision for our Commonwealth and country? Please contribute to make sure he gets re-elected to the Senate and can keep fighting for all of us.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=1lIf5cMQIJuFCML%2FAl6DKsef%2FV0z6XcF)**

Thanks!

Jenny Nadicksbernd

name: Russ Feingold, LegitAction
subject: Let ex-felons vote
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend,

The right to vote is not a luxury or a reward, handed out by the
government as it sees fit. It is the most fundamental right of any
democracy, granting it legitimacy by instilling power in the people
and not in politicians.

That’s why ending the historic wrong of felon disenfranchisement
absolutely must be part of our agenda.

In Florida alone, felon disenfranchisement bars nearly one in four
African-Americans from voting. In tight elections, that
disenfranchisement can make or break an outcome. In 2000, an estimated
600,000 ex-felons were prevented from voting -- while President Bush
won the state by a mere 537 votes.

This is just one of the many reasons why we need a new 21st Century
Voting Rights Act to preserve our democratic legitimacy.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/21st-VRA-em?t=1002&akid=148.356062.Ph8CW9 ]If you haven’t yet joined our call for a new 21st Century Voting
Rights Act, add your name today to combat injustices like felon
disenfranchisement.

Since the Civil War, states have intentionally denied the right to
vote to a certain category of citizens: those with a felony
conviction.

It was invented after slavery, when white elites sought to diminish
the power of newly freed slaves. White politicians tied
disenfranchisement only to those crimes believed to be
disproportionately committed by black people.

Today, felon disenfranchisement bars roughly 6.1 million citizens from
the ballot box -- and 1 in 13 African-Americans nationally. This
denial of a right so inherent to democracy is not based on respect for
the law, but is rather a historical and deliberate effort to prevent
minority voting.

A democracy can’t be legitimate when it systematically targets one
class of people to exclude from elections.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/21st-VRA-em?t=1003&akid=148.356062.Ph8CW9 ]Help combat assaults on democratic legitimacy from issues like
felon disenfranchisement. Sign on to our call for a 21st Century
Voting Rights Act today.

Felon disenfranchisement has nothing to do with the law and everything
to do with politics. It is an excuse to deny voting rights, just like
voter ID laws, and the literacy tests and poll taxes of old. We must
combat it -- or it will compromise legitimacy at the expense of all of
us.

Thank you for standing up for our democratic legitimacy today,

Russ Feingold

name: Tom Perez
subject: Add your name if you're ready to elect Democrats:
disclaimer_text: paid for by cutting working families off from the care they need
body:
Last night, because of millions of grassroots Democrats like you who banded together and fought against it, Republicans' attempt to take health care away from millions of Americans failed again.
---
[****](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954dbd2/2b07114c/32676717/73112e6b/1811399536/VEsH/ "http://my.democrats.org/DSCC-v1?source=em_20170718_tp_rollout")

Friend --

Last night, because of millions of grassroots Democrats like you who banded together and fought against it, Republicans' attempt to take health care away from millions of Americans failed again.

While this is a victory for basic human decency, for the families who rely on the Affordable Care Act, and for all of us who believe that health care is a right for all Americans, the fight is far from over.

Republicans are determined to take away health care from millions. We saw it when Donald Trump, Mitch McConnell, and Senate Republicans worked in secret, behind closed doors, to write a bill that would have given massive tax cuts to millionaires and billionaires, paid for by cutting working families off from the care they need.

They ignored the overwhelming opposition of their constituents -- and now we're going to make sure their constituents throw them out of office.

**[Help build the foundation to take on these Republicans in every state, elect Democrats who will protect every American's access to health care, and take back our majorities in Congress. Say you're in right now.](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954dbd2/2b07114c/32676717/73112e6b/1811399536/VEsE/ "http://my.democrats.org/DSCC-v1?source=em_20170718_tp_rollout")**

Thank you, sincerely, for everything you've done to stop health care repeal. Onward as we continue to fight for the values we believe in.

Tom

Tom Perez
Chair
Democratic National Committee
